info.description: RESTful API for TeslaMate-backed vehicle, charge, drive, statistics, chart, timeline, and insight data. Original TeslaMateApi routes remain compatible; redesigned extension routes may introduce breaking changes. Date query parameters support RFC3339, timezone offsets, decoded-space offsets, local datetime, and date-only formats. When using `+08:00` in URLs, prefer `%2B08:00`, though decoded-space offsets are also accepted.
info.title: TeslaMateApi

get.description: Returns all cars. Nullable DB fields may appear as empty string or 0 in JSON. Legacy: some failures still return HTTP 200 with JSON body containing only an error string field.
Returns the matching car in data.cars (usually one item). Legacy: some failures still return HTTP 200 with JSON body containing only an error string field.
Original compatible battery-health endpoint. Use `/v1/cars/{CarID}/series/battery?metrics=range,soc` for chart-friendly battery series.
Unified calendar endpoint for drives/charges metrics. Supports day, week, month buckets and returns summary + item list.

parameters.description: Supports RFC3339, offset values, decoded-space offsets, local datetime, and date-only values
parameters.name: startDate

parameters.description: Supports RFC3339, offset values, decoded-space offsets, local datetime, and date-only values
parameters.name: endDate

get.description: Registered only when ENABLE_COMMANDS=true. Tesla account access and refresh tokens are never exposed in API responses.

post.description: Registered only when ENABLE_COMMANDS=true and the specific command is allowlisted. Tesla account access and refresh tokens are never exposed in API responses.

get.description: Aggregated dashboard payload for app home. Returns current vehicle snapshot, statistics, calendar summary, chart series, distributions, insights, recent drives, recent charges, recent updates, and warnings in one request.
Charge distribution buckets for start hour, weekday, energy, duration, power and cost. Buckets are ordered and include zero-count gaps.
Drive distribution buckets for start hour, weekday, distance, duration, speed and efficiency. Buckets are ordered and include zero-count gaps.

parameters.description: Supports RFC3339, offset values, decoded-space offsets, local datetime, and date-only values
parameters.name: startDate

parameters.description: Supports RFC3339, offset values, decoded-space offsets, local datetime, and date-only values
parameters.name: endDate

get.description: Baseline-comparison insights generated from current range versus previous equivalent range, including efficiency, cost, charging, battery and anomaly signals.
Aggregated locations payload for TeslaMate location dashboards. Combines drive start/end places and charging places with event counts, coordinates, charge energy, cost, and last-seen timestamps.
Registered only when ENABLE_COMMANDS=true. Returns allowlisted logging commands only.

put.description: Registered only when ENABLE_COMMANDS=true and the logging command is allowlisted.

get.description: Visited geo points and map bounds for heatmap/coverage rendering.
Battery time series for state of charge and rated range.
Charge time series with charge-only metrics and chart metadata.
Drive time series with drive-only metrics and chart metadata.
State-derived time series for parking energy / vampire drain.
Unified period statistics with explicit overview/drive/charge/battery sections, including efficiency, cost, energy and parking metrics.
Canonical range summary for a car. Returns stable overview, driving, charging, parking, battery, efficiency, cost, quality and state sections without legacy include-driven sparse fields.

parameters.description: custom range start; when present endDate is required
parameters.name: startDate

parameters.description: custom range end; when present startDate is required
parameters.name: endDate

get.description: Unified timeline feed for drive/charge/state activities with pagination.

post.description: Registered only when ENABLE_COMMANDS=true and COMMANDS_WAKE, COMMANDS_ALL, or COMMANDS_ALLOWLIST allows /wake_up. Tesla account access and refresh tokens are never exposed in API responses.
